task_completion.description: Generate a 3D visualization of the skull from the CT scan.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 3D model of a human skull (cranium) should be visible in the main 3D viewer pane of the medical imaging software.

task_completion.description: Set the 3D bone surface color to red.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D skull model in the viewer must be distinctly colored red.

task_completion.description: Set the background of the 3D visualization to solid white.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The background behind the 3D skull model in the viewer pane must be pure, solid white.

task_completion.description: Hide the 3D bounding box and reference axes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: There should be no wireframe bounding box surrounding the skull, and no X/Y/Z directional reference axes visible in the 3D viewer pane.

task_completion.description: Orient the camera to an anterior (frontal) view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D skull should be facing directly forward towards the camera, showing the face/front of the cranium.

task_completion.description: Export the result as a PNG to /home/ga/Documents/illustration_base.png.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog, file manager, or terminal showing the file 'illustration_base.png' successfully saved in the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the medical imaging software GUI to generate the visualization.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the medical imaging software (InVesalius) interface, such as adjusting surface properties, colors, and camera views, rather than using terminal scripts to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The exported image is authentically generated from the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final exported image should visually match the 3D viewer pane seen in earlier steps, rather than being a downloaded image from a web browser.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a 2D image editor to fake the result.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no evidence of the agent opening GIMP, Pinta, or other 2D image editors to manually paint the skull red or erase the background/axes.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires manipulating the InVesalius 0051 CT Cranium dataset. The final output must be saved to '/home/ga/Documents/illustration_base.png' with minimum dimensions of 400x400 pixels. The background must be solid white (RGB values near [255, 255, 255] in all corners) and the object must be dominantly red (Red channel significantly higher than Green and Blue channels).
